Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Austin, Texas (relocated from Chicago in 2022); grew up in suburban Midwest, white-collar professional culture; works for a mid-size SaaS company headquartered in San Francisco with hybrid remote setup
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class; Director of Finance at 'NimbusLedger Inc.' (a 380-employee fintech SaaS); base salary $182,000 + RSUs; manages a team of 7 (3 senior analysts, 4 junior); reports directly to the CFO — a known high-value target with broad access to payroll, vendor banking details, and M&A documents in Google Drive and Slack #finance-leadership
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Centrist-leaning fiscally moderate, socially liberal; lapsed Catholic, now functionally secular; values professional integrity and meritocracy; quietly skeptical of corporate DEI initiatives but never voices it in Slack; donates annually to St. Jude and the EFF
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Professionally measured in email and formal Slack channels; dry, mildly sarcastic in DMs with peers (e.g., 'lol another reorg deck'); uses 'gentle reminder' and 'circling back' unironically; rarely uses emojis except 👍 and 🙏; tone shifts to terse/clipped when stressed near quarter-close
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, neutral General American dialect with light Midwestern vowel traces; fluent business-finance jargon (EBITDA, ARR, burn multiple, NRR); conversational Spanish (B1) from college, never used at work
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Mid-career plateau / pre-burnout; married 9 years, two kids (ages 6 and 3); recently took on caretaking role for aging father (early-stage dementia) — visible in calendar as recurring Thursday 4pm 'Dad - call neurologist' blocks; mortgage-stressed after 2024 home purchase; actively but quietly interviewing at two competitors (Friday 'dentist' calendar blocks correlate with recruiter calls) — making him a distracted, credential-reusing, emotionally fatigued weakest-link candidate

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 14 Pro (purchased Sept 2022, now ~3.5 years old, battery health 82%); iOS 18.4, auto-updates enabled but frequently deferred 'tonight'; work email (Outlook) and personal Gmail both configured; Slack, Google Authenticator, and 1Password installed but 1Password vault not used for work logins (uses iCloud Keychain instead); personal Apple ID also signed in on work-issued MacBook — cross-contaminating credential surfaces
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Work: MacBook Pro 14" M2 (company-issued, managed via Jamf, FileVault on, Crowdstrike Falcon agent installed); primary browser Google Chrome (signed into personal Google account for bookmarks sync — against policy but unenforced), with Outlook desktop, Slack desktop, Notion, Excel for Mac, and a personal Spotify install. Personal: 2019 Dell XPS 15 running Windows 11 Home (rarely patched, Edge default but Chrome installed); occasionally accesses work Gmail/Slack via personal browser when traveling
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Low-to-moderate awareness, finance-professional baseline; uses corporate Zscaler VPN only when prompted (often dismisses the nag for 'internal-only' Slack/Drive sessions); no personal VPN; never clears cookies manually — relies on Chrome's default behavior; reuses a 4-variant password family (base word + year + symbol) across LinkedIn, Spotify, his kids' pediatric portal, and — critically — an old NimbusLedger SSO recovery email; SMS-based 2FA on personal accounts, Okta Verify push on work (approves prompts within ~2 seconds without reading, classic MFA-fatigue victim)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Heavy and sloppy: Google Drive (work) auto-syncs Desktop and Documents folders — including a 'personal_taxes_2025' folder accidentally placed inside the synced tree; iCloud Drive syncs iPhone photos and a 'Scans' folder containing photographed driver's license, passport, and a 2024 offer letter from a competitor; Dropbox (personal, free tier, 94% full) holds old W-2s and his father's medical POA PDF; Slack file-sharing used liberally — vendor ACH change forms and a payroll reconciliation .xlsx sent in DMs to the CFO last quarter, still searchable
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe. Personal Gmail: 47,318 unread / 52,104 total (banner just reads '99+'); Work Outlook: ~340 unread in Inbox, uses 'Focused' tab and ignores 'Other'; Slack: 38 unread channels, 6 unread DMs persistent for 4+ days; iPhone lock screen shows 14–22 stacked notifications by EOD; phishing-simulation emails from IT's KnowBe4 reported correctly only 2 of last 9 times (caught the obvious ones, missed the 'DocuSign — CFO requested signature' lure in March)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Generally polished in long-form email (uses Grammarly browser extension on work Chrome — another data exfil surface); in Slack DMs drops capitalization and apostrophes ('cant make 3pm', 'pls send the deck'); habitual 'teh' → autocorrected, 'recieve' slips through; tends to send a follow-up '*the' correction message ~30 seconds after a typo; uses em-dashes inconsistently; signs work emails 'Thanks, —M' but personal emails 'Mike'; voice-to-text used heavily while commuting/caretaking, producing recognizable artifacts ('comma' typed literally, dropped homophones like 'there/their') that make his authored text fingerprintable across channels

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderate-low (4/10). Pragmatic and process-oriented; prefers established finance workflows (NetSuite, Excel pivot tables) over experimenting with new tools. Will click a 'new BambooHR integration' or 'updated DocuSign workflow' link without scrutiny because novelty arrives through legitimate-seeming SaaS channels he already trusts. Low intellectual curiosity about security mechanics — treats Crowdstrike alerts as IT's problem.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Surface-high, eroding (6/10, declining). Externally disciplined: color-coded calendar, inbox-zero attempts, quarterly close checklists. But conscientiousness is collapsing under caretaking load — calendar shows 3 rescheduled 1:1s in past 2 weeks, Slack reminders unsnoozed, and the 'review vendor banking changes' SOP step is increasingly rubber-stamped. Attacker exploit: ACH-change social engineering during quarter-close week when his attention-to-detail drops measurably.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate-low (4/10). Introvert-leaning ambivert; drained by all-hands and offsites, energized by small-group analyst syncs. Prefers async Slack over Zoom. Implication for attacker: responds faster and less critically to text-based (Slack DM, email) lures than to voice/video, and is unlikely to call to verify a wire request.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: High (8/10). Conflict-averse, accommodating to authority — especially the CFO. Will 'just handle it' rather than push back on an unusual request from a senior. Has a documented Slack pattern of approving CFO asks within 4 minutes, even after hours. Prime target for CEO/CFO-impersonation BEC ('Hey Mike, quiet ask — can you push this wire before EOD? In a board prep, can't talk').
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High and rising (7.5/10). Financial stress (mortgage, two kids in daycare/school), caretaker grief (father's dementia), and covert job-hunting anxiety stack into a chronically elevated cortisol baseline. Manifests as MFA-fatigue tapping, skimmed-email decisions, and emotional decision-shortcutting near deadlines. Attacker exploit: time lures to Thursday afternoons (post-neurologist call) and Friday mornings (post-'dentist'/recruiter call) when emotional regulation is depleted.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion-adjacent / Ego-preservation via Career Anxiety. He is quietly interviewing and terrified of (a) his employer discovering it and (b) the competitor offers falling through before he can escape. A spear-phish themed as 'LinkedIn InMail from [Competitor] Recruiting — final round scheduling' or 'Confidential — your offer letter (DocuSign)' would bypass his usual skepticism because acknowledging it requires admitting the job search exists. He will not report it to IT for fear of triggering DLP review of his outbound traffic.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Money / Mortgage Pressure. The 2024 home purchase left him cash-thin; RSU vesting cliff is 8 months out. Lures referencing 'RSU early-liquidity program,' 'Carta tender offer,' '401(k) emergency hardship withdrawal,' or 'property tax refund — Travis County' will pull him into credential-harvesting flows. Secondary financial lever also opens vendor-payment fraud: he personally knows the AP rhythm and will approve a $48K ACH redirect if framed as a known vendor's 'updated banking — Q2 audit requirement.'
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Professional irrelevance — fears being labeled 'not strategic' and bypassed for VP-Finance; (b) being publicly outed as job-hunting before he has a signed offer; (c) his father's medical/financial information leaking (POA PDF in Dropbox is the unspoken nightmare); (d) accidentally causing a SOX or audit finding under his name; (e) low-grade paranoia about being on a layoff list after the Q1 reorg deck leaked. Attacker can weaponize (a) and (d) with 'URGENT: audit exception flagged on your cost center' lures that drive panic-clicks.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (1) Not being present for his kids — late closes and a missed kindergarten recital in April still sting; a lure framed as 'school portal — emergency contact update required' or 'pediatric portal password reset' hits a guilt nerve and reuses his known password family. (2) Shame at considering moving his father to memory care; any 'eldercare benefits enrollment' or 'long-term care insurance update' email is opened immediately. (3) Quiet shame over the personal Apple ID on the work MacBook and Chrome-sync-against-policy — he will NOT escalate a suspicious event to IT if doing so risks exposing his own policy violations, giving the attacker a critical non-reporting window of 24–72 hours post-compromise. (4) Guilt about the lapsed Catholicism — minor, but 'St. Jude donor receipt' is a trusted-sender pattern he never scrutinizes.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Moderate morning-leaning, sleep-deprived. Natural wake 5:45–6:15 AM CT (Apple Watch sleep data syncs to iCloud; visible in Health app exports). First Slack 'active' green dot typically pings 6:32–6:48 AM from mobile while making the kids' breakfast. Goes to sleep 11:15 PM–12:10 AM — pushed later by post-bedtime catchup work and (Thu/Fri) recruiter-related email drafting. Sleep debt averages 1.8 hours/night per his Oura ring (subscription on personal Amex, ring synced to work MacBook via Bluetooth — a credential-bridging side channel). Attacker window: 5:50–6:45 AM lures hit a pre-coffee, phone-only, MFA-fatigued state where he taps Approve without reading.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Nominal 8:30 AM–6:00 PM CT hybrid; in-office Tue/Wed at NimbusLedger's Austin satellite (300 W 6th St), WFH Mon/Thu/Fri. Real schedule is bimodal: heads-down 8:30 AM–12:30 PM, school-pickup gap 3:00–3:45 PM (calendar blocked 'School run — DO NOT BOOK'), then a second shift 8:30 PM–10:45 PM after kids' bedtime where he clears Slack DMs and approves Coupa/Ramp/Bill.com requests. Quarter-close weeks (last 5 business days of Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec) extend to 1:00 AM and weekends. Friday afternoons are softest — 'dentist' blocks 1:00–2:30 PM correlate with recruiter Zooms, and he's mentally checked-out from NimbusLedger work 3:00 PM onward.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Drives a 2022 Honda Pilot from Mueller neighborhood to downtown Austin office on Tue/Wed, ~22 minutes each way via I-35 (28–40 min with traffic). CarPlay-connected; listens to Acquired podcast, All-In, and Bloomberg Surveillance — never audiobooks, never music during work commute. Phone is locked but notifications read aloud through Siri, including Slack DM previews and Gmail subject lines (huge OSINT leak vector: attacker-crafted subject lines weaponize the audio channel). Genuinely offline 6:55–7:18 AM outbound (in shower/breakfast handoff) and 6:10–6:45 PM inbound (school pickup, no phone interaction). WFH days: zero commute, replaced by a 7:15 AM Peloton ride (15 min) during which phone is on charger across the room — narrowest offline window of his day.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Sharp behavioral delta. Weekdays: Slack/Gmail responsive within 8 minutes 7 AM–10 PM. Saturdays: phone in 'Family' Focus mode 9 AM–4 PM, Slack notifications silenced, but personal Gmail and iMessage still active; Costco run 10–11 AM (location pings Cedar Park store), soccer practice 11:30 AM. Sunday evenings 8:00–10:30 PM is 'Sunday Scaries prep mode' — he opens Slack, scrolls #finance-leadership, and reviews the Monday board-prep deck in Drive. This Sunday-evening session is his single highest-risk window: caffeinated, anxious, alone, no IT support online, and he is psychologically primed to act on 'urgent Monday' framing. A Sunday 9:14 PM 'CFO: quick fav before tomorrow' DM is nearly optimal.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three predictable doomscroll windows visible in Screen Time exports (iCloud-synced, also mirrored to a Family Sharing account his wife can see — he is therefore *more* careful about adult content but *less* careful about phishing links, which look like 'work'): (a) 6:20–6:55 AM bathroom scroll — LinkedIn feed, Blind app (anonymous tech-worker forum where he lurks under handle 'austin_fin_41'), Apple News finance tab; (b) 12:35–1:05 PM lunch scroll at desk — Reddit r/fatFIRE, r/personalfinance, Twitter/X finance accounts; (c) 10:50–11:40 PM bed scroll — Zillow (mortgage-refi obsession), Glassdoor (target-company salary research), and recruiter InMails. The Blind handle is the highest-value OSINT target: he has posted enough complaints about 'my CFO' and 'our Series D dilution math' to triangulate NimbusLedger and his identity within 3 posts.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuine zero-footprint windows are rare and identifiable. Reliable dark windows: 7:00–7:20 AM (shower/kids), 6:10–6:45 PM (school pickup + dinner prep), 8:00–8:30 PM (kid bedtime story — phone in kitchen), 11:45 PM–5:45 AM (sleep, though phone on nightstand and Slack 'Do Not Disturb' is set but MFA push notifications still wake the screen). Declared Focus Blocks on his work calendar ('Deep Work — Forecast Model' Mon/Wed 9:30–11:30 AM) are NOT true offline blocks — he keeps Slack open and responds within 6 minutes to anything from the CFO or his direct reports, breaking his own focus protocol roughly 11 times per block. Attacker implication: there is no 2+ hour window where he is reliably unreachable, meaning *any* sent lure will be seen within 15 minutes during 6 AM–11 PM, and the only true safe-from-him interval for lateral movement inside compromised accounts is 12:30 AM–5:30 AM CT.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear household of 4 in a 2024-purchased 4BR in Northwest Hills, Austin: wife Jennifer 'Jen' Halloran-Reese (39, part-time UX researcher at a healthtech startup, primary parent during his late closes), son Caleb (6, kindergarten at Hill Country Elementary, recent ADHD eval in progress), daughter Nora (3, full-time at Bright Horizons daycare — $2,400/mo line item visible in shared Mint export). Extended/dependent: father Robert 'Bob' Reese (72, early-stage dementia, lives alone in Naperville IL in the family home; Mike holds financial POA — scanned PDF in his personal Dropbox folder 'Dad_Legal/'); mother deceased (2019, pancreatic cancer); one younger sister Allison Reese-Vogel (38, lives in Denver, RN, primary in-person caretaker for Bob, ongoing low-grade resentment over caretaking imbalance visible in their iMessage thread synced to MacBook). In-laws Tom & Patty Halloran (Round Rock TX, retired, watch the kids Tuesday evenings). No pets after the family dog 'Biscuit' died Nov 2025.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact.identity: Rachel Vandermeer, Senior Recruiter at 'Stonefork Partners' (boutique fintech executive-search firm retained by two NimbusLedger competitors — Vellum Finance and Arclight Capital Systems)
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.nature_of_relationship: Active covert recruiting relationship since February 2026. She placed him into final-round loops at both competitors; one verbal offer pending, one second-round scheduled. NOT an affair, but functionally treated by Mike with the same operational secrecy — burner-adjacent behavior patterns.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.communication_hygiene: Mike uses a personal Gmail alias (m.reese85.careers@gmail.com) created Jan 2026, accessed ONLY from his personal iPhone and the personal Chrome profile on his work MacBook (policy violation #1). Calendar holds on Fridays 11am–12pm labeled 'dentist' or 'PT — knee' correlate 1:1 with her Calendly links. He has never mentioned her name in Slack, work email, or any synced channel. Two LinkedIn InMails from her sit unread-flagged so they appear unopened to LinkedIn's read-receipt telemetry.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.attacker_exploitation_path: This is the single highest-leverage attack vector in the entire profile. A spear-phish spoofing 'rachel.vandermeer@stonefork-partners.co' (lookalike TLD) with subject 'Re: Arclight — final round + comp details (CONFIDENTIAL)' and a DocuSign-themed credential harvester will (a) be opened within minutes regardless of time of day, (b) be opened on the work MacBook because the personal Chrome profile is there, (c) harvest credentials he reuses across personal Gmail, iCloud, and — critically — his NimbusLedger Okta password (confirmed reuse pattern from a 2023 LinkedIn breach corpus showing 'Caleb2019!' variants), and (d) NEVER be reported to NimbusLedger IT because reporting it requires disclosing the job search, the personal Chrome profile, and the alias email. Estimated non-reporting window: 48–96 hours, sufficient for full lateral movement to the #finance-leadership Slack channel and the CFO's shared Drive folder containing the M&A diligence pack.

The 'Hidden' Contact.collateral_secrets_in_same_silo: Same hidden silo also contains: (i) Signal thread with sister Allison re: moving Dad to memory care (~$9k/mo, contentious); (ii) a Schwab brokerage login where he's been quietly modeling RSU early-exercise scenarios; (iii) one unsent draft resignation email in Gmail Drafts dated 2026-04-30.
